root_text.an10.116:1.1: Atha kho ajito paribbājako yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an10.116:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho ajito paribbājako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an10.116:2.2: Tena pañcamattāni cittaṭṭhānasatāni cintitāni, yehi aññatitthiyā upāraddhāva jānanti upāraddhasmā”ti.
root_text.an10.116:3.4: yaṁ bhagavā bhāseyya, bhagavato sutvā bhikkhū dhāressantī”ti.
root_text.an10.116:4.1: “Tena hi, bhikkhave, suṇātha, sādhukaṁ manasi karotha, bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an10.116:4.2: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an10.116:5.1: “Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco adhammikena vādena adhammikaṁ vādaṁ abhiniggaṇhāti abhinippīḷeti, tena ca adhammikaṁ parisaṁ rañjeti.
root_text.an10.116:5.2: Tena sā adhammikā parisā uccāsaddamahāsaddā hoti:
root_text.an10.116:6.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco adhammikena vādena dhammikaṁ vādaṁ abhiniggaṇhāti abhinippīḷeti, tena ca adhammikaṁ parisaṁ rañjeti.
root_text.an10.116:6.2: Tena sā adhammikā parisā uccāsaddamahāsaddā hoti:
root_text.an10.116:7.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco adhammikena vādena dhammikañca vādaṁ adhammikañca vādaṁ abhiniggaṇhāti abhinippīḷeti, tena ca adhammikaṁ parisaṁ rañjeti.
root_text.an10.116:7.2: Tena sā adhammikā parisā uccāsaddamahāsaddā hoti:
root_text.an10.116:8.1: Adhammo ca, bhikkhave, veditabbo dhammo ca;
root_text.an10.116:8.3: Adhammañca viditvā dhammañca, anatthañca viditvā atthañca yathā dhammo yathā attho tathā paṭipajjitabbaṁ.
root_text.an10.116:9.1: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, adhammo, katamo ca dhammo, katamo ca anattho, katamo ca attho?
root_text.an10.116:9.4: ye ca micchādiṭṭhipaccayā aneke pāpakā akusalā dhammā sambhavanti, ayaṁ anattho;
root_text.an10.116:9.5: sammādiṭṭhipaccayā ca aneke kusalā dhammā bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchanti, ayaṁ attho.
root_text.an10.116:11.3: ye ca micchāvimuttipaccayā aneke pāpakā akusalā dhammā sambhavanti, ayaṁ anattho;
root_text.an10.116:11.4: sammāvimuttipaccayā ca aneke kusalā dhammā bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchanti, ayaṁ attho.
root_text.an10.116:12.3: Adhammañca viditvā dhammañca, anatthañca viditvā atthañca yathā dhammo yathā attho tathā paṭipajjitabban’ti,
root_text.an10.116:12.4: iti yaṁ taṁ vuttaṁ, idametaṁ paṭicca vuttan”ti.

translation_text.an10.116:1.1: Then the wanderer Ajita went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an10.116:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an10.116:2.1: “Worthy Gotama, we have a spiritual companion called ‘Astute’.
translation_text.an10.116:2.2: He has worked out around five hundred arguments by which followers of other religions will know when they’ve been refuted.”
translation_text.an10.116:3.1: Then the Buddha said to the mendicants,
translation_text.an10.116:3.2: “Mendicants, do you remember this Astute’s points?”
translation_text.an10.116:3.3: “Now is the time, Blessed One! Now is the time, Holy One!
translation_text.an10.116:3.4: Let the Buddha speak and the mendicants will remember it.”
translation_text.an10.116:4.1: “Well then, mendicants, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an10.116:5.1: “Mendicants, take a certain someone who rebuts and quashes unprincipled statements with unprincipled statements. This delights an unprincipled assembly,
translation_text.an10.116:5.3: ‘He is truly astute! He is truly astute!’
translation_text.an10.116:6.1: Someone else rebuts and quashes principled statements with unprincipled statements. This delights an unprincipled assembly,
translation_text.an10.116:6.3: ‘He is truly astute! He is truly astute!’
translation_text.an10.116:7.1: Someone else rebuts and quashes principled and unprincipled statements with unprincipled statements. This delights an unprincipled assembly,
translation_text.an10.116:7.3: ‘He is truly astute! He is truly astute!’
translation_text.an10.116:8.1: Mendicants, you should know bad principles and good principles.
translation_text.an10.116:8.2: And you should know bad results and good results.
translation_text.an10.116:8.3: Knowing these things, your practice should follow the good principles with good results.
translation_text.an10.116:9.1: So what are bad principles? What are good principles? What are bad results? And what are good results?
translation_text.an10.116:9.2: Wrong view is a bad principle.
translation_text.an10.116:9.3: Right view is a good principle.
translation_text.an10.116:9.4: And the many bad, unskillful qualities produced by wrong view are bad results.
translation_text.an10.116:9.5: And the many skillful qualities fully developed because of right view are good results.
translation_text.an10.116:10.1: Wrong thought is a bad principle.
translation_text.an10.116:10.2: Right thought is a good principle. …
translation_text.an10.116:10.3: Wrong speech is a bad principle.
translation_text.an10.116:10.4: Right speech is a good principle. …
translation_text.an10.116:10.5: Wrong action is a bad principle.
translation_text.an10.116:10.6: Right action is a good principle. …
translation_text.an10.116:10.7: Wrong livelihood is a bad principle.
translation_text.an10.116:10.8: Right livelihood is a good principle. …
translation_text.an10.116:10.9: Wrong effort is a bad principle.
translation_text.an10.116:10.10: Right effort is a good principle. …
translation_text.an10.116:10.11: Wrong mindfulness is a bad principle.
translation_text.an10.116:10.12: Right mindfulness is a good principle. …
translation_text.an10.116:10.13: Wrong immersion is a bad principle.
translation_text.an10.116:10.14: Right immersion is a good principle. …
translation_text.an10.116:10.15: Wrong knowledge is a bad principle.
translation_text.an10.116:10.16: Right knowledge is a good principle. …
translation_text.an10.116:11.1: Wrong freedom is a bad principle.
translation_text.an10.116:11.2: Right freedom is a good principle.
translation_text.an10.116:11.3: And the many bad, unskillful qualities produced by wrong freedom are bad results.
translation_text.an10.116:11.4: And the many skillful qualities fully developed because of right freedom are good results.
translation_text.an10.116:12.1: ‘You should know bad principles and good principles.
translation_text.an10.116:12.2: And you should know bad results and good results.
translation_text.an10.116:12.3: Knowing these things, your practice should follow the good principles with good results.’
translation_text.an10.116:12.4: That’s what I said, and this is why I said it.”

variant_text.an10.116:2.2: upāraddhāva jānanti → upāraddhā pajānanti (bj) | upāraddhasmā”ti → upāraddhamhāti (bj, pts1ed)
